title: Goldman Sachs Upgrades AMD Stock Rating on Agentic AI Tailwinds
text: Goldman Sachs upgraded Advanced Micro Devices stock to Buy with a $450 price target from Hold at $240, citing agentic AI tailwinds and strong datacenter GPU growth ahead. The firm hiked its price target on shares to $450 from $240, implying 27% upside. Advanced Micro Devices has considerable room to run over the next year, largely due to structural agentic artificial intelligence tailwinds following a strong first-quarter report. The investment bank upgraded the chipmaker to buy from hold following AMD's blowout earnings report. Their price target is built on a 30x P/E multiple applied to a normalized EPS estimate of $15. The upgrade reflects Goldman Sachs' confidence in AMD's ability to capitalize on growing demand for server CPUs and AI infrastructure. This signals institutional confidence in AMD's competitive positioning against NVIDIA in the evolving AI market, particularly as enterprise customers diversify their GPU sourcing.
